reviews.review_msg: Attend office hours!

Worth delaying your graduation to take his lectures. Took 320, 321 and 420 with him. You will gain a much deeper understanding of accounting just by attending his lectures. Questions are encouraged and he answers them flawlessly. The course material is brutal but he somehow makes it interesting. Amazing lecturer and a wonderful human being

Robin is one of the best profs out there, defo take a class with him if you can. Grade is mostly based on exams, which are very time-constrained with low averages. To practice, do the end of chapter questions based on the stuff he tells you to study (very transparent with exams). Keep doing it over and over until you can do it quickly & correctly!

She was a good teacher. She cares about you and wants to help. I understand where the bad reviews come from, however I think the classes she teaches are just difficult so she gets a bad rep. If you do all her practice questions (in class and textbook) you will do well. She may seem strict if you have a conversation with her she's very  sweet.
She is an awesome person but not a great teacher. Her tests are borderline unfair and no one ever finishes them. If you are taking her then do all the examples, practice questions, and start praying.

She has little knowledge of M&A or any of the other topics she teaches, she uses hypothetical scenarios to create her own problems which would never occur in real life. Her lectures are extremely difficult to follow given she speaks in such random and various tones.

Stay as far away as possible, unless you want to be docked randomly

She really cares and is really nice but wow, this is a difficult course. Her exams are on the harder end but since it's curved it balances out. Make sure to do her examples and the homework. She explains the concepts and her examples really well in class and answers questions..

She's a nice person but her exams has too many surprises. One minute you're doing consolidations, next minute she's asking you to calculate ratios on the exam, like ??  The questions are also super long so there isn't enough time. If you think you can beat the curve then go for it, I just wouldn't want to do it again.

Kim is a very nice prof, just not the best for teaching Consolidation..  It's a tough course and she gets confused a lot while teaching, which confuses the entire class. Be ready to teach yourself material.  Attend all classes though, she does provide format which helps a lot. Homework checkattendance every class.   TL;DR: Nice but confusing

Maureen is a very intelligent professor, and an excellent communicator. Although Business Combinations is not an easy course to teach, Maureen generally does a good job articulating course concepts. She does a detailed run through on a few textbook problems in class, which really helps with your learning! You have to work hard nonetheless.

She is so intelligent and one of the best profs there are at SFU! She is so cute the way she explains things with her hand gestures and most importantly, she doesn't follow the text book method if she knows an easier way. Going to class will make your life easier and make sure to ask questions if you're confused cuz she does make challenging exams

Her slides are horrible and confusing, she doesn't follow the textbook, and she doesn't provide full examples yet still expects you to understand her way of completing problems!  She is a really nice lady and can tell that she's passionate about the stuff.  But she really needs to provide better notes.
She is a good and caring professor. Attend the lectures, do the hw questions. Her slides are good to study for the exams. No surprises in the exams.
Do the practice questions she assigns in class then you will do well on her exams

Arsineh did a really good job of teaching the course online for the first time. I studied what she recommended maybe only a week before the midterm/final and ended up doing fine by making detailed excel templates and notes for each chapter. Do the assigned hw, study what she tells you, and rewatch her lectures for clarification, and you'll be fine!

All genuine. Arsineh is not the best at teaching online, but this is a difficult time for both students and profs. However, this is how I succeeded in this course: REIVEW ALL NOTES, REVIEW TEXTBOOK SECTIONS SHE MENTIONS ONLY, AND DO ALL ASSIGNED PROBLEMS. I did not pre read nor did I do extra practice problems. Open book + recorded lectures also?!

She is a very kind professor. I think midterm is much harder than final exam, final exam do not need to do the excel. I got a low mark on midterm below average, but final exam do better, get b+. Reading textbook is very useful before lecture, but there are too many materials in textbook.

This is a great course, you don't become a CFO, but you understand accounting and finances, I'm not sure about the bad reviews, most likely people who did not want to do the work , She's awesome and will attend her classes again.

Amazing professor. Cares for his students, knows everyone's name somehow, and really knows his stuff. Makes challenging concepts relatively simple with great lecture examples. Highly recommend, would not take 420 with anyone else. Tests are basically what he does in lecture, it's just some complicated stuff; do the practice and you will do great.
Jamal is very clear teaching 420 and makes a "hard" class very easy by the way he explains things. If you keep up with homework and practice questions, you can definitely do well in this class.
He ok.

Jamal is the best professor I have ever had! The course is known to be difficult. However, Jamal provides clear notes and explains the concepts extremely well. He is also always available to provide extra help. Only lazy students do not do well in this course. If you do your homework and study, you will succeed. Jamal is amazing!
